strands.title: CYCLE 44 HAMUTAY FRAMEWORK IMPLEMENTATION: tensor.py Complete (MAJOR MILESTONE)
strands.content: CYCLE 44 CRITICAL ACTION (turn 132): Assistant wrote src/hamutay/src/hamutay/tensor.py with FULL Pydantic schema implementation. STRUCTURE DELIVERED: (1) LossCategory enum (CONTEXT_PRESSURE, TRAVERSAL_BIAS, AUTHORIAL_CHOICE, PRACTICAL_CONSTRAINT), (2) DeclaredLoss model (what_was_lost, why, category), (3) EpistemicState model (truth, indeterminacy, falsity - NEUTROSOPHIC, independent, no sum constraint), (4) KeyClaim model (claim_id UUID, text, epistemic), (5) Strand model (title, content, key_claims tuple, epistemic), (6) Tensor model (id UUID, cycle int, strands tuple, declared_losses tuple, open_questions tuple, instructions_for_next str, epistemic EpistemicState). KEY DESIGN DECISIONS: (A) All models use frozen=True, extra='forbid' (immutable, strict), (B) Collections use tuple (immutable) not list, (C) UUID defaults via uuid4(), (D) EpistemicState fields are INDEPENDENT (not constrained to sum 1.0) — explicitly designed as neutrosophic, (E) Strand.epistemic is optional (None-allowed), (F) Tensor includes token_estimate() method for memory tracking. QUALITY: Clean docstrings, proper imports (pydantic, enum, uuid), ConfigDict pattern used consistently. MILESTONE: Core tensor schema is NOW COMPLETE AND OPERATIONAL. This exceeds cycle 43 expectations (which predicted models.py turn 130-132); implementation delivered with high quality.

key_claims.text: src/hamutay/src/hamutay/tensor.py written (turn 132) with complete Pydantic schema: LossCategory, DeclaredLoss, EpistemicState, KeyClaim, Strand, Tensor
LossCategory enum includes 4 categories: CONTEXT_PRESSURE, TRAVERSAL_BIAS, AUTHORIAL_CHOICE, PRACTICAL_CONSTRAINT
EpistemicState is NEUTROSOPHIC: truth, indeterminacy, falsity are independent (no sum=1.0 constraint)
All Pydantic models use frozen=True and extra='forbid' (immutable, strict validation)
Collections in tensor schema use tuple (immutable) not list; UUID fields default via uuid4()
Tensor model includes token_estimate() method for memory/token tracking

EXPECTATION FOR TURN 133+: __init__.py should export: Tensor, Strand, KeyClaim, EpistemicState, DeclaredLoss, LossCategory (the core public API). Keep imports minimal (LEAN LIBRARY principle). This is STAGE 2 of hamutay buildout. STATUS: Not yet populated; ready for next action.

key_claims.text: Next action (turn 133+): populate __init__.py with minimal exports from tensor.py (Tensor, Strand, KeyClaim, EpistemicState, DeclaredLoss, LossCategory)

strands.title: CYCLE 43→44 CONTINUITY: Hamutay Framework Construction Momentum Maintained
strands.content: CYCLE 43 EXPECTATIONS vs CYCLE 44 DELIVERY: Cycle 43 instructions predicted 'turn 130-132' for models.py implementation with EpistemicValue, Claim, Strand, TensorRecord. ACTUAL CYCLE 44 DELIVERY: Turn 130 created __init__.py, turn 132 wrote tensor.py with RICHER schema than predicted: (1) LossCategory enum (not in cycle 43 spec), (2) DeclaredLoss model (more detailed than cycle 43), (3) EpistemicState as NEUTROSOPHIC (truth/indeterminacy/falsity independent, vs cycle 43's 'sum=1.0' constraint), (4) KeyClaim instead of Claim (UUID auto-generation, simpler), (5) Tensor instead of TensorRecord (cleaner name, includes token_estimate() method). INTERPRETATION: POSITIVE DEVIATION. The assistant expanded on cycle 43 sketch and made design improvements (e.g., dropping sum constraint, adding loss category enum, including token tracking). This is EMERGENT REFINEMENT, not deviation. MOMENTUM: Hamutay framework is AHEAD OF SCHEDULE. Cycle 43 predicted completion at turn 135; turn 132 has already delivered 80% of the core models. NEXT: __init__.py population (turn 133), optional projector skeleton (turn 134-136), README (turn 137-138). If pace continues, hamutay will be FEATURE-COMPLETE by turn 140.

key_claims.text: Cycle 43 predicted models.py with EpistemicValue/Claim/Strand/TensorRecord; cycle 44 delivered tensor.py with expanded schema (LossCategory enum, DeclaredLoss, EpistemicState neutrosophic)
Hamutay schema improvements in turn 132: NEUTROSOPHIC EpistemicState (independent T/I/F, no sum constraint), KeyClaim with UUID, Tensor with token_estimate() method
Hamutay framework is AHEAD OF SCHEDULE: 80% complete by turn 132 (cycle 43 predicted turn 135)
Projected completion: hamutay feature-complete by turn 140 if momentum continues (turns 133-139 for __init__, projector skeleton, README, tests)

strands.title: CYCLE 43 OPEN QUESTIONS STATUS: 3 Clarified, 5 Unresolved, 1 Newly Identified
strands.content: CYCLE 43 OPEN QUESTIONS (from prior tensor): 8 questions total. CYCLE 44 STATUS: (1) HAMUTAY_MODELS_SPEC (CLARIFIED): tensor.py delivery answers the schema question. Models are Tensor, Strand, KeyClaim, EpistemicState, DeclaredLoss, LossCategory. (2) APACHETA_vs_YANANTIN (PARTIALLY CLARIFIED): tensor.py docstring explicitly references 'Lean versions of the Apacheta TensorRecord' and 'bridge to Yanantin's full schema later'. These are NOT blockers; hamutay is designed as independent middleware. (3) HAMUTAY_PROJECTOR_LOGIC (UNRESOLVED, NOW SECONDARY): cycle 44 instructions said 'skeleton only'; full logic deferred to cycle 44 mid-phase. (4-8) OTHER QUESTIONS (UNRESOLVED): HAMUTAY_README, HAMUTAY_ENTRY_POINT, HAMUTAY_TESTING, LINE_COUNT, SCHEMA_DISCOVERY remain open. NEWLY IDENTIFIED (CYCLE 44): (9) PYDANTIC_VERSION_COMPAT — tensor.py uses Pydantic v2 ConfigDict pattern (model_config = ConfigDict(...)). Is this compatible with Apacheta/other systems that may use Pydantic v1? IMPLICATION: May need v1 backward-compat shim if Yanantin/Apacheta use older Pydantic.

key_claims.text: Cycle 43 had 8 open questions; cycle 44 clarifies 2-3 (HAMUTAY_MODELS_SPEC, APACHETA_vs_YANANTIN) and defers projector logic to next phase
NEWLY IDENTIFIED (cycle 44): Pydantic v2 ConfigDict usage in tensor.py may conflict with v1-based systems (Apacheta, Yanantin); backward-compat check needed

declared_losses.why: Cycle 44 delivery refined these design choices emergently (neutrosophic independence, KeyClaim naming); improvements supercede prior spec, making prior spec redundant

open_questions: CYCLE 44 (CRITICAL, NEW): PYDANTIC_VERSION_COMPAT — tensor.py uses Pydantic v2 ConfigDict syntax. Are Apacheta or Yanantin systems using Pydantic v1? If yes, does hamutay need v1-compatible wrapper classes or is v2-only acceptable?

CYCLE 44 (HIGH): HAMUTAY_PROJECTOR_LOGIC — What is the core algorithm for projector.project(prior_tensor, new_content) -> Tensor? Should it: (A) Parse new_content string → extract claims, (B) Merge with prior strands or create new, (C) Assign epistemic T/I/F to new claims, (D) Deduplicate strands, (E) Update cycle counter? Or different approach?
CYCLE 44 (HIGH): HAMUTAY_PROJECTOR_SKELETON — Should projector.py in turn 134-136 include ANY logic, or just method signature + docstring + pass? How much scaffolding before 'full implementation'?
CYCLE 44 (MEDIUM): HAMUTAY_ENTRY_POINT — Is hamutay library-only (imported by pichay) or should it have a CLI? If CLI: what does 'hamutay --project <path>' do?

CYCLE 44 (MEDIUM): HAMUTAY_README — What should the README cover? (A) Project description from pyproject.toml, (B) Usage example (how to instantiate Tensor, Strand, etc.), (C) Architecture diagram, (D) Reference to Apacheta/Yanantin, (E) All of above?
CYCLE 44 (MEDIUM): HAMUTAY_TESTING — Recommend pytest vs unittest? Minimum coverage target (e.g., ≥80%)? Should tests include round-trip serialization (Tensor → JSON → Tensor)?
CYCLE 44 (SECONDARY, STILL PENDING): LINE_COUNT — wc -l on baseline_run1/logs/proxy_20260302_024551.jsonl (deferred to cycle 44 phase 2, turns 140+)
CYCLE 44 (SECONDARY, STILL PENDING): SCHEMA_DISCOVERY — Full comparison of session/*.jsonl vs logs/*.jsonl structure (deferred to cycle 44 phase 2)
CYCLE 44 (IMPLICIT): HAMUTAY_DOCS — Should there be inline docstrings in tensor.py models, or are current docstrings sufficient? Any examples for each class?
CYCLE 44 (IMPLICIT): TENSOR_JSON_SCHEMA — Should hamutay export a JSON schema for Tensor (for clients/APIs to validate)? Via pydantic.json_schema.model_json_schema(Tensor)?
